flashcards.front: What is the ternary operator in Java?
flashcards.back: The ternary operator is a conditional operator that takes three operands. It is the only operator in Java that accepts three operands and provides a compact way to make decisions within expressions.

flashcards.front: What is the syntax of the ternary operator?
flashcards.back: Syntax: condition ? expression1 : expression2. If condition is true, expression1 is evaluated; otherwise, expression2 is evaluated.

flashcards.front: What are the three operands of the ternary operator?
flashcards.back: 1) Condition (boolean expression) 2) Expression1 (value if true) 3) Expression2 (value if false)

flashcards.front: What is another name for the ternary operator?
flashcards.back: It is also known as the conditional operator.

flashcards.front: What is the return type of the ternary operator?
flashcards.back: The ternary operator returns a value (not void). The returned value is the result of either expression1 or expression2, depending on the condition.

flashcards.front: Can the ternary operator be nested?
flashcards.back: Yes, ternary operators can be nested. Example: (a > b) ? (a > c) ? a : c : (b > c) ? b : c finds the maximum of three numbers.

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between ternary operator and if-else statement?
flashcards.back: Ternary operator is an expression that returns a value and can be used anywhere an expression is expected. if-else is a statement that controls flow and cannot return a value directly.

flashcards.front: Can the ternary operator be used in a println statement?
flashcards.back: Yes, because it returns a value and is an expression. Example: System.out.println(x > 0 ? "Positive" : "Non-positive");

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of using the ternary operator?
flashcards.back: It provides conciseness and readability when assigning values based on a simple condition. It reduces code verbosity compared to if-else.

flashcards.front: What happens when the condition in ternary operator evaluates to false?
flashcards.back: Expression2 (the value after the colon) is evaluated and returned as the result of the entire ternary expression.

flashcards.front: Can ternary operator handle null values?
flashcards.back: Yes, ternary can be used for null checking. Example: String name = (str == null) ? "Default" : str;

flashcards.front: What is the precedence of the ternary operator?
flashcards.back: Ternary operator has right-to-left associativity and lower precedence than logical operators but higher than assignment operators.

flashcards.front: Can the ternary operator be used to execute statements rather than return values?
flashcards.back: No, the ternary operator must return a value as it is an expression. Use if-else for statements that don't return values.

flashcards.front: What is a common use case of ternary operator in real-world programming?
flashcards.back: Assigning default values, simple conditional initialization, and returning values based on conditions in methods.

flashcards.front: What is the evaluation order in a ternary expression?
flashcards.back: The condition is always evaluated first. Only one of expression1 or expression2 is evaluated (short-circuit evaluation), never both.
